description_long: This apartment building features a rectangular, block-like structure with a flat roof and a repetitive grid of windows framed by brick and concrete. The facade is dominated by rows of evenly spaced windows, each bordered by reddish-brown brickwork and separated by gray concrete bands. The lower portion of the building is clad in larger, horizontally aligned concrete panels, giving it a sturdy base. A small staircase leads up to a single main entrance at the center of the front facade, suggesting access to the interior. The building lacks balconies or decorative elements, emphasizing a utilitarian and functional design. The overall appearance is consistent with mid-20th-century urban residential architecture, prioritizing practicality and efficient use of space.
description: A rectangular, multi-story apartment building with a grid of brick-framed windows and a concrete base, featuring a single main entrance and a flat roof. The design is utilitarian and symmetrical, with no balconies or ornate details.
description_view_0: The side view shows the repetitive window grid and concrete base, with no visible entrance or distinguishing features.
description_view_1: Another side view, nearly identical to view 0, with the same window and concrete panel arrangement and no entrance visible.
description_view_2: The front view displays the main entrance with a small staircase, centered among the symmetrical rows of windows and the prominent concrete base.
description_view_3: The opposite side view mirrors views 0 and 1, showing the same window pattern and concrete base without an entrance.
attribute_window_arrangement: The windows are arranged in a highly regular, symmetrical grid pattern, with equal spacing both vertically and horizontally across all visible facades.
